channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bill Clinton discharged after hospitalization for flu
item.description: Former President Clinton was discharged Tuesday morning after being hospitalized a day earlier for flu, according to his spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Eye drops recalled nationwide due to fungal contamination
item.description: The recall was issued after a consumer complained of a "foreign material" they found in a sealed vial of the eye drops.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, NewsNation

item.title: California residents on edge as high surf and flooding threats persist on Christmas Eve
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — Persistent high surf and flooding threats along California’s coast had residents on high alert a day after a major storm was blamed for one man’s death and the partial collapse of a pier, which propelled three people into the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Seafarers move global trade — and Christmas gifts. These ministries bring them holiday cheer
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — On a balmy December evening, four Indian crew members, eager to go explore this Mediterranean city where their container ship docked for a few hours, turned for help to Stella Maris.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL’ORTO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Nikki Glaser uses Prime Video's NFL postgame show appearances to help prepare for Golden Globes
item.description: INGLEWOOD, Calif. (AP) — What do Tom Brady, Taylor Swift and comedy have to do with learning about football? In the case of Nikki Glaser, it is all intertwined. Glaser has become a familiar face to football fans this season.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: France has a new government, again. Politics and crushing debt complicate next steps
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s president and prime minister managed to form a new government just in time for the holidays. Now comes the hard part.
item.dc:creator: ANGELA CHARLTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Walmart illegally opened bank accounts for 1 million drivers, lawsuit alleges
item.description: Walmart and fintech company Branch Messenger are being sued by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for allegedly forcing more than one million delivery workers to use expensive deposit accounts to access their paychecks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American Airlines experiences 'technical issue' with all flights on Christmas Eve
item.description: American Airlines is reportedly experiencing delays on the morning of Christmas Eve due to what the airline called a "technical issue.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Jones, KRON4

item.title: Technical issue grounds American Airlines flights for 1 hour on Christmas Eve
item.description: A nationwide ground stop was in place for about an hour for all American Airlines flights on Christmas Eve morning. "American Airlines reported a technical issue this morning and requested a nationwide ground stop.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Major storm pounds California's central coast, blamed for man's death and partially collapsing pier
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A major storm pounded California’s central coast on Monday, bringing flooding and high surf that was blamed for fatally trapping a man beneath debris on a beach and later partially collapsing a pier, tossing three people into the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI investigating after 'unruly' passenger dies aboard Royal Caribbean cruise ship
item.description: An investigation has been opened by the FBI after a guest aboard a Royal Caribbean cruise ship died shortly after leaving the port of San Pedro in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Officer fatally shot in a North Carolina supermarket, suspect in custody, police say
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (AP) — A police officer responding to a report of a man with a gun inside a North Carolina supermarket was fatally shot Monday and a suspect was later taken into custody, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inside the state and federal charges against the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A triptych of criminal charges paints a searing, sometimes disparate portrait of the man accused of ambushing and killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO Brian Thompson as the executive arrived at a Manhattan hotel for his company’s annual investor conference.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates before Trump can resume executions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday announced that he is commuting the sentences of 37 of the 40 people on federal death row, converting their punishments to life imprisonment just weeks before President-elect Donald Trump, an outspoken proponent of expanding capital punishment, takes of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Where is Santa? How to track his Christmas 2024 journey
item.description: Santa Claus, St. Nicholas, Babbo Natale, Père Noël — whatever you call him, the man clad in red will be here soon.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Bill Clinton admitted to hospital with fever
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted to a Washington hospital on Monday with a fever, his spokesperson said on social media.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Judge rules Arkansas law allowing criminal charges against librarians is unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge has struck down key parts of an Arkansas law that would have allowed criminal charges against librarians and booksellers for providing so-called “harmful” materials to minors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bill Clinton hospitalized with fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to Georgetown University Medical Center in Washington after developing a fever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Adnan Syed's lawyers seek to reduce his prison sentence
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Attorneys for Adnan Syed, whose complex legal case was chronicled in the hit podcast “Serial," are seeking to have his prison sentence reduced as he awaits further court decisions.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden’s death row commutations draws chorus of mixed reactions
item.description: A chorus of supporters and critics have emerged following the commutation of nearly every federal death row inmate by President Biden, whose decision came at a time when he’s trying to frame a so-far troubled legacy.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump again calls to buy Greenland after eyeing Canada and the Panama Canal
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — First it was Canada, then the Panama Canal. Now, Donald Trump again wants Greenland. The president-elect is renewing unsuccessful calls he made during his first term for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules, state and federal laws, outlining allegations of prostitution, statutory rape and illicit drug use, among other accusations.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Congress looks to ban Chinese drones widely used in US: What to know about the debate
item.description: Lawmakers in Washington are seeking to ban new sales of drones from two dominant Chinese manufacturers, arguing they could be used to spy on Americans and that the low-cost models are hurting the U.S. drone industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Taylor Swift makes $250,000 donation to Kansas City nonprofit
item.description: The global superstar made a $250,000 donation to Operation Breakthrough, a nonprofit that helps families in the city's urban core.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Rousseau, NewsNation

item.title: Chat with Santa or find out if you are on the naughty list with these tech features
item.description: You can access the new (and limited!) Santa Chat feature through the ChatGPT app by starting a new chat, hitting the live chat button.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, NewsNation

item.title: Live Updates: Greensboro police officer killed in shooting at Food Lion on Lawndale Drive
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (WGHP) — A Greensboro police officer has died after a shooting that took place at a Food Lion in Greensboro on Monday, according to the Greensboro Police Department. At 10:45 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, NewsNation

item.title: The Container Store files for bankruptcy as sales slump
item.description: The Container Store joins the growing list of companies that have filed for bankruptcy this year, citing major economic challenges.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, NewsNation

item.title: Tech expert warns of device that disables Wi-Fi cameras
item.description: It just takes a flick of a switch, and it can make your Wi-Fi camera stop working and miss the crucial video you need.
item.dc:creator: Elena Cousino, NewsNation

item.title: Audit reveals at least $56 million in improper Indiana Medicaid payments
item.description: (WXIN/WTTV) — Indiana leaders are calling for "urgent" reforms after a federal audit revealed the state has made at least $56 million in improper Medicaid payments for Hoosiers with autism. A recently published audit from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, NewsNation

item.title: Calif. restaurant closes amid ‘Ladies Night’ lawsuit
item.description: Lima Restaurant's chef/owner John Marquez says the lawsuit has cost his restaurant tens of thousands of dollars in legal fees.
item.dc:creator: Jack Molmud, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants mass deportations. A ride-along with immigration officers shows the challenges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The immigration officers sat in their vehicles before dawn near a two-story building. A New York subway line rumbled overhead, then an officer's voice crackled over the radio. After watching for about two hours, he said, “I think that's Tango,” using a term for target. “Gray hoodie.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chat with Santa or find out if you are on the naughty list with these tech features
item.description: We've moved past simply mailing your wish list to the North Pole. Santa has arrived on ChatGPT and you can talk to him.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KRON4

item.title: The new right gathers to celebrate Trump and the splintering of the Republican Party
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — As Washington heaved over the possibility of a partial government shutdown, leading far-right figures gathered with thousands of Donald Trump’s most ardent supporters and, for the most part, gloried in splintering the president-elect’s party.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4
